content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real correctness defects. One allows non-canonical prompt content to expand the planned-elsewhere routing allowlist, and the other can spend the sibling detail scan budget on closed candidates and hide later open siblings.

## Votes

- `fr-21-70e017d4`: Accept
  `extract_bullet_bead_ids_from_section` treats any line whose trimmed text equals `## Nearby work` or `## Already Planned Elsewhere` as a real section start, then keeps parsing `- ...` bullets until the next column-1 `## ` heading. It does not track fences and it does not require a top-level heading. `render_bead_task_prompt` meanwhile deliberately rewrites canonical-looking headings in AGENTS guidance to indented lines and leaves fenced examples intact. That mismatch means indented or fenced examples can be misparsed as authoritative nearby/planned-elsewhere sections, injecting arbitrary bead IDs into the allowlist later consumed by review/final_review. Restricting extraction to top-level canonical sections outside fenced blocks is the correct fix.
- `fr-21-e45be213`: Accept
  The sibling prefetch path passes summary and relation status into `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted_for_prefetch`, but that helper ignores both parameters and only filters out unavailable IDs or siblings whose detail is already loaded as closed/deferred. For siblings discovered only via `parent_detail.dependents`, render mode later requires a loaded detail when no summary exists, and the render filter does honor summary/relation/detail status. With the current `take(...)` and `max_detail_loads` caps, a run of closed parent-child siblings can consume the prefetch budget before later open siblings are loaded, so the final nearby section can omit valid open siblings. Using summary/relation status during prefetch, or continuing to scan until five non-closed candidates are retained, is technically warranted.
